A Gashadokuro is a colossal humanoid skeleton standing 50 ft tall at level 1. The bones are dense and heavy, colored an off-white with a faint grey shimmer. There is no flesh, no skin, no connective tissue of any kind. The bones are held together and animated purely by the force of the spirit trapped within. Each individual bone is as hard as forged steel and as thick as a tree trunk, interconnected by invisible force rather than ligaments. When the Gashadokuro moves, the bones grind against each other with a sound like rocks tumbling down a cliffside. The skull is proportionally large, with deep hollow eye sockets that emit a dim grey-white glow. The jaw hangs slightly open and never closes. The ribcage is wide enough for a fully grown person to stand inside without ducking. The arms are slightly too long compared to the legs, giving it a hunched gait, and each hand ends in five fingers tipped with jagged splinters of bone instead of nails. The spine is a series of vertebrae the size of wagon wheels. The whole body radiates a low vibration, a constant subsonic hum that you feel in your teeth before you hear it.

Gashadokuro are incredibly aggressive and will attack anything that moves. They have no concept of mercy, fear, pain, or self-preservation. They cannot be reasoned with, cannot be frightened, and cannot be negotiated with. A Gashadokuro that sees a living creature will pursue it forever, crashing through forests and buildings without slowing down, until either the creature is dead or the Gashadokuro is. This single-minded rage makes them predictable in the worst way: you know exactly what it is going to do, and that is kill you, and there is nothing you can do to stop it from trying.

No one knows exactly what causes a Gashadokuro to form. They appear in places where enormous numbers of people died in a short time and were left unburied. Mass battlefields where the dead were not collected, cities wiped out by plague and abandoned, slave pits where bodies were left to rot. Not every mass grave spawns one. The specific conditions are not understood. What is known is that once a Gashadokuro forms, it does not stop. It does not age, it does not weaken, and it cannot reproduce. There is no mating, no egg-laying, no spawning. Every Gashadokuro that exists is a unique event.

A Gashadokuro gains 1 level per year of existence. It grows about 2 ft taller and gains roughly 4,000 lbs of bone mass per level. The oldest recorded Gashadokuro was estimated to be level 300 and stood nearly 650 ft tall. It took three full armies working together to bring it down. Most Gashadokuro are killed long before they reach that age because they cannot be ignored. A 50 ft skeleton walking through a village gets noticed. When a Gashadokuro is killed, the invisible force holding its bones together dissipates and the skeleton collapses into a massive pile. Every single bone is recoverable as Gashadokuro Bone, a material on par with dragon bone in quality and surpassing it in sheer quantity due to the creature's size. A level 1 Gashadokuro produces roughly 35,000 lbs of usable bone.
###### Unique Abilities
- **Bone Crusher (Active, 1 Ap):** The Gashadokuro picks up a creature of size Large or smaller within its reach. The target must make a STR save (DC 15 + Affinity) or be Grappled. While grappled, at the start of each of the Gashadokuro's turns, the target takes 6d8 + Affinity bludgeoning damage as the Gashadokuro squeezes. The target remains grappled until it succeeds on a STR save to break free (DC 15 + Affinity, repeat at the start of each of its turns).
- **Tremor Stomp (Active, 4 Mana):** The Gashadokuro stomps the ground with one foot. All creatures within 30 ft must make a DEX save (DC 10 + Affinity) or take 2d8 + Affinity bludgeoning damage and be knocked Prone. Creatures that succeed take half damage and are not knocked Prone. Structures and objects in the area take double damage.
- **Skull Volley (Active, 3 Mana):** The Gashadokuro rips chunks of bone from its own body and hurls them in a 20 ft radius at a point within 60 ft. All creatures in the area must make a DEX save (DC 10 + Affinity) or take 2d8 + Affinity bludgeoning damage. The Gashadokuro takes 1d6 true damage from tearing off its own bone but the bone that was lost quickly regenerates.
- **Colossal Rampage (Passive):** The Gashadokuro can move through objects and structures as if they were difficult terrain. When it enters a space occupied by an object or structure, the object takes 6d8 bludgeoning damage. Living creatures it moves through must make a DEX save (DC 10 + Affinity) or take 3d8 bludgeoning damage and be pushed aside.
- **Undying Giant (Passive):** A Gashadokuro cannot take Health damage by any means until its Vitality is totally exhausted. Any effect which would bypass Vitality to harm Health directly is instead applied to Vitality until no Vitality remains. 
- **Daunting Presence (Passive, Aura 60 ft):** Any creature that starts its turn within 60 ft of the Gashadokuro must make a WIS save (DC 10 + Affinity) or become Frightened for 1 turn. A creature that succeeds is immune to this ability for 24 hours.
- **Stronger Leveling (Passive):** Instead of gaining +1 Gen Dmg Mod each level the Gashadokuro gains +5. 

**Standard Passives:** Hardened Body, Damage Threshold, Stable Core, Crushing Blows, Executioner, Scratch Resistance, Counterstrike, Fast Action (L5+), Double Timer
**Standard Actives:** Slam, Heavy Strike, Multi Attack, Charge, Push Wave
### Statblock/Growth Table/Harvestable Materials for Gashadokuro
> For more information on how to level up a monster see [[7.1 Monsters]].

The stat block below represents a level 1 Gashadokuro. Higher level Gashadokuro gain Vitality, Health, Mana, and stats as described in the leveling rules. A Gashadokuro cannot breed, cannot evolve, and exists only as a unique entity in a given location.
